mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns show positive correlation with FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that reduce consumer spending expectations and trigger negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 60% of instances when UNRATE crosses the 0.2pp threshold over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal consumption expenditures and stable consumer price inflation indicates robust demand without overheating, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative forward returns in more than 55% of triggered cases across rolling 2-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: An increase in the index of industrial production reflects strengthening manufacturing output and capacity utilization, which drives positive short-term S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive 5-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.02 during recovery periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 2.5%, monetary policy restricts liquidity and raises borrowing costs for corporations, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are statistically indistinguishable from zero when FEDFUNDS remains above 2.5% for consecutive months.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls combined with a declining unemployment rate confirms broad-based labor market strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 days.
failure_condition: The combined labor signal yields negative forward returns in over 50% of observations during a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: Higher housing starts indicate increased construction activity and consumer confidence in long-term wealth, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: HOUST increases correlate with negative 20-day forward returns in more than 55% of monthly observations over a rolling 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals immediate supply-side contraction and reduced corporate output, triggering negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns remain positive in 60% of cases when INDPRO drops below the 0.5% threshold over a 3-year rolling window.
